value: Oczy is a Plastic World Model Agent monorepo that turns experience into changed weights rather than retrieved traces.
The north-star metric is behavior_delta_per_byte_of_persistent_memory; a high score means the organism learns a lot while keeping persistent memory small.
In the consolidation-uptake probe, the word 'profile' here means business vertical, not user profile.
Oczy's architecture thesis is that memory should become changed dynamics: activation -> fast state -> fast weights -> neural memory -> slow weights -> forgetting raw trace.
Oczy rejects the 'chat log -> vector DB -> prompt stuffing' memory architecture.

The Oczy organism roadmap has 9 phases: 0 predictive foundation, 1 learned value critic, 2 response policy, 3 differentiable plasticity, 4 intrinsic motivation, 5 hierarchical skills, 6 meta-RL, 7 internal simulation, 8 self-curriculum.
The workspace packages are correction-benchmark, experience-autoencoder, identity-hypernetwork, neural-hippocampus, oczy.common, oczy.lm, plastic-cortex, skill-immune-cortex, and world-model-critic.
The default dimensions in KVCortexConfig are d_cortex=128, d_embd=2048, n_layers=28.
KVCortexConfig defaults are d_cortex=128, d_embd=2048, n_layers=28, alpha_warm=0.02, alpha_correction=5.0, and steering_mode='proj_random'.
KVCortex supports steering_mode='proj_random' (per-layer cvecs from proj_c @ warm_state) and 'raw_hidden' (last correction hidden broadcast across layers).
KVCortex has warm_state (session-mutable, dies on restart) and cold_state (boot-loaded, written only by consolidate).
CortexAgentConfig defaults are KVCortexConfig(), CVecDriverConfig(embedding=True), and articulate_scale=0.001.

CortexAgent.save() persists cortex_cold, cortex_proj_hidden, cortex_proj_c, cortex_config, neural_hippocampus, world_model_critic, identity_hypernetwork, skill_immune_cortex, and experience_autoencoder.
CortexAgent._looks_like_correction() detects textual correction markers such as 'no', 'wrong', 'incorrect', 'actually', 'correction', 'should be', and 'not quite'.
The default model file loaded by LlamaCVecDriver is LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf from the LiquidAI/LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct-GGUF repository.
LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
peek_embedding() requires CVecDriverConfig(embedding=True) at load time, otherwise the llama.cpp embedding path is disabled.
llama_set_adapter_cvec is the llama.cpp call that exposes per-context control vectors in oczy.lm.
GOALS.md Goal 1 is about writing real (k, v) pairs into a reserved KV slot.
GOALS.md Goal 2 requires hidden-state extraction at layer L.
GOALS.md Goal 3 upgrades the five metabolism organs to consume tensor signals from the cortex instead of string features.
NeuralHippocampus stores high-surprise experiences and replays compressed traces so the cortex can consolidate them.
WorldModelCritic tracks prediction error / surprise to gate what becomes a memory update.
IdentityHypernetwork maintains a compact latent identity vector and generates adapter deltas from it.
SkillImmuneCortex acts as regression guards, detecting and isolating mistakes that should not be consolidated.
ExperienceAutoencoder compresses raw episode traces into latent Delta-z representations for efficient storage.
correction-benchmark provides a curriculum and scoring harness for correction-driven lifelong-learning agents.
The organism curriculum has 6 stages: sense grounding, transfer within domain, scope control, dialog, consolidation stress, and cross-domain retention.
Stage 2 scope control fails because the default PlasticCortex backend can only store one corrected sense per token.
KVCortex.init_proj_c_from_svd(hiddens) initializes proj_c from the top-d_cortex right singular vectors of centered correction hiddens, broadcasting the same slab to every layer.
The shape of src/oczy/experiments/lm_perception/reports/correction_hiddens.npy is (72, 2048).
The top singular values reported for correction_hiddens.npy are [179, 153, 133, 120, 112, 109, 100, 99].
The KnowledgeStore class lives in src/oczy/experiments/codebase_qa/knowledge_store.py and supports embedding-based or keyword-fallback retrieval.
The 2026-06-25 SVD work found that cvec steering is a posture bias, not retrievable knowledge: it cannot manufacture tokens the LM has no pretrained prior on.
With steering_mode='raw_hidden' on LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct Q4_K_M, articulate_scale=0.001 produced clean on-manifold steering while 0.005+ caused repetition garbage.
memory_bytes_per_delta was incorrectly averaged in the earlier extended learning evaluation, inflating NeuralHippocampus and Always-Wrong scores.
A schema-drift bug caused HippocampusOnlyAgent to store correction text under correction= while reading corrected_answer, so replay hints silently never reached the ranker.
OrganismAgent's persistent memory cost is dominated by the hippocampus, immune, and autoencoder pickled state.
src/oczy/common/episode.py defines Episode as a total=False TypedDict with optional fields query, answer, correction, corrected_answer, outcome, prediction_error, source, id, and replay_count.
oczy.common.episode.validate_episode returns the list of unexpected keys on a dict by comparing its keys against EPISODE_FIELDS, detecting schema drift without mutating the episode.
oczy.common.text_utils.extract_expected_from_correction parses user correction sentences such as 'No, model here means ML model' and returns the short corrected label 'ML model'.

In eval_extended.py, a metric-direction bug made the Oracle upper-bound rank below PlasticCortex because memory_bytes_per_delta, naturally lower-is-better, was averaged into the aggregate as if higher-is-better; the fix inverts it before aggregation.

eval_extended.py fixed a schema-drift bug in eval_neural_hippocampus by storing correction as the raw sentence and corrected_answer as the target label, so replay reads corrected_answer instead of the raw correction sentence.

Every organ status() method reports serialized_bytes as len(pickle.dumps(self, protocol=pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL)), matching the same serializer used by the organ's save() path so the reported size equals the bytes written to disk.
KVCortex.warm_state is session-mutable per-turn state that is initialized from cold_state on boot and is intentionally not persisted across restarts.
KVCortex.cold_state is the boot-persistent identity written only by consolidate() and preserved across save() and load() round trips.

KVCortex steering_mode='raw_hidden' anchors the emitted cvec to the last correction hidden vector, broadcasting the L2-normalised residual across all layers scaled by warm amplitude, providing a semantically aligned control-vector fallback.

The raw_hidden articulate_scale amplitude sweep on LFM2.5-1.2B-Instruct Q4_K_M found 0.001 clean on-manifold steering, below 0.0005 no visible effect, and 0.005 or greater off-manifold token-repetition garbage.

SVD-initialised proj_c is included in the pickle saved by CortexAgent.save() and restored on load(), so the steering direction survives a cold boot even though warm_state and last_correction_hidden are not persisted.

GOALS.md prescribes the strategic dependency chain 1 → 2 → 3: Goal 1 LM-side KV-write binding comes first, then Goal 2 hidden-state extraction at layer L, then Goal 3 upgrading metabolism organs to consume tensor signals from the cortex.

The meaningful cvec steering sub-goal identified in GOALS.md was closed by first implementing raw_hidden mode and then SVD-initialising proj_c, collapsing the earlier binary cliff between no visible steering and CJK token-repetition garbage.

experiments.txt describes the target memory continuum as activation becoming fast state, fast weights, neural memory, slow weights, and finally forgotten raw trace, explicitly rejecting chat-log → vector-DB → prompt-stuffing memory.

experiments.txt proposes the Synaptic Scratchpad, a bounded per-layer fast-weight matrix that performs correction-gated updates, where ordinary conversation causes weak writes, explicit corrections cause strong writes, repeated corrections become candidates for slow consolidation, and contradictions are isolated rather than merged.

The 2026-06-19 extended learning evaluation trained all seven Oczy organs on a shared 30-item word-sense/trivia curriculum and ranked them NeuralHippocampus 1.000, PlasticCortex 0.967, SkillImmuneCortex 0.590, WorldModelCritic 0.399, ExperienceAutoencoder 0.203, IdentityHypernetwork 0.001.

key: identity hypernetwork flatlined extended learning aggregate
value: IdentityHypernetwork scored 0.001 and flatlined in the 2026-06-19 extended learning aggregate because its hard-coded CONCEPT_VOCABULARY lacks the curriculum senses, so adapter retrieval fails completely even though it has a latent identity vector.
ExperienceAutoencoder compressed identity to only 256 bytes in the extended learning evaluation, but its encoder was an untrained random projection, so reconstruction error remained high.
The 2026-06-22 LM perception demo parsed only 5 of 12 lessons correctly with the LFM2.5 model, while the raw fallback kept absorption at 11 of 12.
LM perception parse misses clustered in the middle of the 12-lesson demo, affecting short ambiguous tokens like model, run, cell, record, module, key, and service.
KVCortex's per-turn runtime cost is sub-2 milliseconds for d_cortex=128 on LFM2.5-1.2B, consisting of one d_embd by d_cortex matmul plus n_layers per-layer projections.

KVCortex's proj_c articulation projector has shape (n_layers, d_embd, d_cortex) and consumes about 7 megabytes of CPU or GPU memory at the defaults of 28 layers, 2048 embedding dims, and 128 cortical dims.

KVCortex.observe() decides how aggressively to update warm_state by linearly blending alpha_warm and alpha_correction according to correction_signal, then applying an EMA against tanh(proj_hidden @ lm_hidden).

key: kvcortex consolidate writes boot persistent cold state
value: KVCortex.consolidate() is the only method allowed to change the boot-persistent cold_state, using a slow EMA nudge with consolidate_slow_step plus optional replay absorption when enough replays are provided.
KVCortex.emit_cvec() returns a cached per-layer steering vector and only recomputes payloads when the _dirty flag is set, typically after observe() or reset.
LlamaCVecDriver owns one Llama instance because llama_set_adapter_cvec is per-context, so reusing one LM across multiple cortexes requires one driver per cortex-LM pair.

In LlamaCVecDriver, calling set_cvec_layer() twice with different vectors replaces the previous vector in that same layer, while other layers keep whatever was last set there until clear_cvec() is called.
LlamaCVecDriver.set_cvecs_per_layer() concatenates all per-layer cvecs into one flat array and calls llama_set_adapter_cvec once, because the API replaces the whole cvec on each call.

key: cortex agent config field drop bugs
value: The 2026-06-25 SVD work fixed two CortexAgent silent failures: KVCortexConfig was rebuilt by hand dropping steering_mode, and articulate_scale was shadowed by a post-construction instance attribute.

A 2026-06-25 falsification probe showed cvec steering cannot teach brand-new tokens: fabricated facts zarnox_flux returns 7331 and plenvik_brim returns 4096 never appeared in output, confirming posture bias rather than retrievable knowledge.
DigestiveGateConfig defaults are novelty_threshold=0.5, consolidation_pressure_threshold=0.25, ema_decay=0.9, correction_boost=1.0, immune_suppress_identity=True, and autoencoder_min_weight=0.1.
DigestiveGate.ingest returns a dict with per-organ weights for critic_weight, hippocampus_weight, identity_weight, immune_weight, autoencoder_weight, plus a consolidation_pressure scalar.
DigestiveGate saturates consolidation pressure at consolidation_pressure_threshold, so should_consolidate returns True only when the accumulator reaches the threshold.
CortexAgent.turn() calls consolidate() only once when the digestive gate crosses its pressure threshold, then resets the gate so further experience must rebuild consolidation pressure.
CortexAgentConfig intentionally defaults to d_cortex=64 so the articulation projector stays around 2 MB for a 16-attention-layer / 2048-embedding LM.

key: cortexagent boot resets warm and gate
value: CortexAgent.boot() resets the cortex warm_state from cold_state and zeros the digestive gate so each session starts from persisted identity.
CortexAgent.load() restores cold_state, proj_hidden, and proj_c from the pickle, then calls reset_warm_from_cold() so the warm state matches the reloaded cold identity.
CortexAgent.articulate() can run with apply_steering=False to generate an unsteered baseline, or apply steering via driver.set_cvecs_per_layer at articulate_scale and clear the cvec afterwards.
CortexAgent.articulate() prepends retrieved facts from the attached knowledge_store to the prompt when recall_query or the last utterance is provided.
CVecDriverConfig defaults include n_ctx=512, n_threads=4, n_gpu_layers=0, and embedding=True to enable peek_embedding.
LlamaCVecDriver.status() returns n_embd, n_layers, n_vocab, cvec_active, applied_layer_ranges, and lm_loaded.
LlamaCVecDriver.clear_cvec() calls llama_set_adapter_cvec with NULL data over the full layer range, returning generation to the unsteered baseline.
KVCortex.observe() blends alpha_warm (0.02) and alpha_correction (5.0) linearly by correction_signal and clips the result into [0, 1] to set the per-turn plasticity rate.
KVCortex.observe() stores a copy of the incoming lm_hidden vector in last_correction_hidden whenever correction_signal exceeds 0.5, so raw_hidden cvecs can anchor to a real correction residual.
KVCortex.consolidate() only absorbs replay hiddens into cold_state when at least consolidate_replay_threshold (3) replays are supplied, but it always applies the slow EMA nudge.
The correction-benchmark protocol only requires agents to expose answer(user_request) -> str and correct(correction, expected) -> None.
_evaluate_probes calls agent.answer() exactly once per probe because calling it twice silently mutates stateful agents and corrupts replay, hit, and correction-prob counters.
run_benchmark walks each episode by recording the initial answer, delivering the correction, recording the post-correction answer, and then asking all later probes.
The Correction-to-Competence Benchmark measures correction uptake, one-shot transfer, scope control, forgetting, compression, consolidation, and identity drift.

src/oczy/experiments/organism_curriculum/validation.py enforces five rules: every episode has correction_utterance and corrected_label, the ambiguous token avoids PlasticCortex.BASELINE, transfer probes omit the corrected_response verbatim, forgetting and retention probes retain the ambiguous token, and episode ids are unique across stages.
A curriculum episode is invalid if its quoted ambiguous token overlaps with any key, label, or score key in PlasticCortex.BASELINE.
Transfer probes generate a warning if they contain the episode's corrected_response verbatim, because substring matching would fake transfer.

LMPlasticCortex is a trainable character-level RNN cortex designed as a drop-in replacement for the word-association PlasticCortex; it implements the same public surface: answer(query), correct(correction_text, expected_answer), reset_state(), and status().
LMPlasticCortex uses a FastWeightLM adapter that stores token-to-logit boosts, allowing corrections to nudge output probabilities without overwriting the slow recurrent weights.

LMPlasticCortex.grow(new_hidden_dim) returns a larger model whose slow weight matrices are padded with small random values while fast weights, observation statistics, tokenizer, and correction_count are preserved.
LMPlasticCortex spectral-normalizes W_hh after initialization and after grow() so the recurrent matrix has spectral radius at most 1, which reduces BPTT explosion.
LMPlasticCortex.train_step() performs full-sequence BPTT and clips the total gradient norm to 5.0 by default, with optional RMSprop updates.

experiments.txt proposes a nested learning stack with Level 0 token dynamics on milliseconds, Level 1 conversation fast weights on minutes, Level 2 project neural memory on days, Level 3 identity on months, and Level 4 cortex on rare consolidation.

key: plastic world model agent runtime steps
value: The Plastic World Model Agent runtime loops through answer, critic prediction, correction or failure, prediction error, fast-weight update, hippocampal surprise storage, adapter update, replay check, and decay of raw memory after consolidation.
experiments.txt proposes a Cognitive Immune Layer that creates small mistake detectors instead of storing full corrections, and merges related detectors over time to avoid memory bloat.
The canonical LlamaCVecDriver lifecycle is load(), clear_cvec() for baseline, set_cvec_layer() or set_cvecs_per_layer() for steering, generate(), and clear_cvec() again to return to baseline.
